Context.purpose: Simplify MCP runtime to a clear FastMCP structure: OAuth Proxy protection only for /mcp, fixed proxy tool set, and root-token admin surface.
Context.scope: Keep existing public tools (search_messages, get_message_context, get_related_messages, list_sources), preserve tg-chat-rag proxy behavior, and keep /admin protected by ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN.

constraints.constraint-1: Public MCP surface remains exactly four tools: search_messages, get_message_context, get_related_messages, list_sources.
constraints.constraint-2: search_messages always injects filters.chat_ids from TG_CHAT_RAG_CHAT_IDS; caller-provided filters.chat_ids stays forbidden.
constraints.constraint-3: All upstream tg-chat-rag calls use Bearer token from TG_CHAT_RAG_BEARER_TOKEN.
constraints.constraint-4: FastMCP OAuth Proxy is enabled only for /mcp transport surface.
constraints.constraint-5: /admin/* is not OAuth-protected; it is protected only by ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN login/session policy.
constraints.constraint-6: ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN must never be accepted for /mcp authentication.
constraints.constraint-7: FastMCP HTTP stream transport is the single MCP transport; endpoint is /mcp.
constraints.constraint-8: Tool parameter schemas stay Zod-based and richly described for deterministic MCP contracts.
constraints.constraint-9: No API-key repository lifecycle is required in this simplified runtime target.
constraints.constraint-10: No internal trip-planning/orchestration subsystem is introduced in this phase.

contract.purpose: Load and validate runtime configuration for FastMCP server, OAuth Proxy upstream credentials, admin auth, and tg-chat-rag proxy calls.

export-loadConfig.PURPOSE: Validate env and return typed AppConfig.

type-AppConfig.PURPOSE: Typed runtime config for OAuth Proxy, tool proxy policy, and admin auth.

contract.purpose: Provide structured logs for runtime startup, auth decisions, tool execution, upstream failures, and admin actions.

export-createLogger.PURPOSE: Create module-scoped logger with semantic block labels.

contract.purpose: Define deterministic Zod schemas and allowlist for the four existing proxied MCP tools.

export-SearchMessagesInputPublic.PURPOSE: Public input schema for search_messages with forbidden filters.chat_ids path.

export-PROXIED_TOOL_NAMES.PURPOSE: Stable allowlist for tool registration and proxy dispatch.

export-TOOL_INPUT_JSON_SCHEMAS.PURPOSE: Tool descriptions used by FastMCP addTool metadata.

contract.purpose: Call tg-chat-rag methods API and normalize protocol/timeouts/errors for proxy handlers.
Create and configure FastMCP OAuthProxy bound to Logto OIDC endpoints for /mcp authentication.

export-createOauthProxy.PURPOSE: Instantiate OAuthProxy with Logto auth/token endpoints and configured scopes.

notes.note-3: Proxy base URL comes from PUBLIC_URL.

contract.purpose: Validate tool input, apply policy normalization, execute upstream methods, and map deterministic MCP tool responses.

export-executeTool.PURPOSE: Main proxy entrypoint for all registered tools.

contract.purpose: Authenticate admin operators with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN and protect /admin routes via signed cookie session checks.

export-authenticateAdmin.PURPOSE: Validate ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN on /admin/login and issue session cookie.

export-clearAdminSession.PURPOSE: Invalidate admin auth cookie on logout.

contract.purpose: Render admin login and ops pages with simple root-token auth workflow.

export-renderAdminLayout.PURPOSE: Render HTML layout for admin pages.

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.NAME: FastMcpRuntime

contract.purpose: Create FastMCP server with OAuth Proxy for /mcp, register fixed tool set, and mount admin routes on Hono app.

export-createFastMcpRuntime.PURPOSE: Build FastMCP with oauth.enabled=true and oauth.proxy wired from M-AUTH-PROXY.

export-registerProxyTools.PURPOSE: Register the four existing tools with described schemas and canAccess guards.

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-AUTH-PROXY, M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-ADMIN-UI

notes.note-1: /mcp is OAuth-protected through FastMCP oauth proxy flow.
notes.note-2: /admin routes are handled separately by AdminAuth flow.

M-SERVER.NAME: ServerMain

contract.purpose: Bootstrap runtime dependencies and start FastMCP HTTP stream server.

export-main.PURPOSE: Load config, create dependencies, start FastMCP runtime, install graceful shutdown.

M-SERVER.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-AUTH-PROXY, M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-ADMIN-UI, M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME

contract.purpose: Call ServerMain and map fatal startup errors to process exit behavior.

export-bootstrap.PURPOSE: Invoke main() and handle fatal startup exceptions.

DF-MCP-OAUTH.NAME: mcp oauth proxy auth flow
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-1: External MCP client reaches /mcp and receives OAuth discovery/register/authorize flow from FastMCP.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-2: FastMCP OAuthProxy exchanges auth code with Logto upstream using pre-registered client credentials.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-3: FastMCP tool execution proceeds only for authenticated session.

DF-SEARCH-MESSAGES.NAME: search messages proxy flow
DF-SEARCH-MESSAGES.step-1: Client invokes search_messages with public args.
DF-SEARCH-MESSAGES.step-2: M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS validates args and blocks filters.chat_ids from caller input.
DF-SEARCH-MESSAGES.step-3: M-TOOL-PROXY injects internal TG_CHAT_RAG_CHAT_IDS and forwards request to tg-chat-rag.
DF-SEARCH-MESSAGES.step-4: Response is normalized into deterministic MCP tool result envelope.

DF-MESSAGE-CONTEXT.NAME: message context proxy flow
DF-MESSAGE-CONTEXT.step-2: Schema validation ensures deterministic payload constraints.
DF-MESSAGE-CONTEXT.step-3: Request is proxied to tg-chat-rag and upstream response is returned in MCP envelope.

DF-RELATED-MESSAGES.NAME: related messages proxy flow
DF-RELATED-MESSAGES.step-2: Schema and allowlist checks run before upstream call.
DF-RELATED-MESSAGES.step-3: Result is proxied and normalized without tool-side business branching.

DF-LIST-SOURCES.NAME: list sources proxy flow
DF-LIST-SOURCES.step-1: Client invokes list_sources with message_uids array.
DF-LIST-SOURCES.step-2: Schema enforces array bounds and non-empty entries.
DF-LIST-SOURCES.step-3: Proxy returns deterministic structured output from upstream.

DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.NAME: admin root token flow
DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.step-2: M-ADMIN-AUTH verifies against ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN and issues session cookie.
DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.step-3: Protected /admin/ops routes require valid admin session and never use OAuth /mcp auth path.

MT-1.NAME: oauth isolation test
MT-1.step-1: Unauthenticated request to /mcp must receive OAuth flow challenge.
MT-1.step-2: Same caller accessing /admin/login must not be processed by OAuth proxy.
MT-1.step-3: No path overlap should allow ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN on /mcp.
MT-1.result: Pass when /mcp and /admin auth paths remain strictly isolated.

MT-2.NAME: tool policy integrity test
MT-2.step-1: Caller sends search_messages with nested filters.chat_ids.
MT-2.step-3: When caller omits chat_ids, runtime injects configured internal chat IDs.
MT-2.result: Pass when public chat scope cannot be overridden externally.

MT-3.NAME: dependency direction test
MT-3.step-1: Trace dependency chain from entrypoints to integrations.
MT-3.step-2: Ensure no module in UTILITY/INTEGRATION depends on ENTRY_POINT modules.
MT-3.step-3: Verify deprecated auth modules are not in active dependency chain.
MT-3.result: Pass when graph is acyclic and top-down.

Phase-1.name: ConfigAndContracts
Phase-1.step-1: Finalize M-CONFIG env contract for OAuth Proxy + admin root token + tg-chat-rag.
Phase-1.step-2: Finalize M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS with field descriptions and deterministic errors.
Phase-1.justification: All runtime branches depend on validated config and stable tool schemas.

Phase-2.name: Integrations
Phase-2.step-1: Implement M-AUTH-PROXY wrapper around FastMCP OAuthProxy.
Phase-2.step-2: Keep M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT focused on one method call contract.
Phase-2.justification: External boundaries should be explicit before wiring runtime entrypoints.

Phase-3.name: CoreLogicAndAdmin
Phase-3.step-1: Simplify M-TOOL-PROXY orchestration around validate -> policy -> upstream -> normalize.
Phase-3.step-2: Keep M-ADMIN-AUTH and M-ADMIN-UI root-token-only with no MCP auth coupling.
Phase-3.justification: Core behavior and admin guardrails are independent and testable in isolation.

Phase-4.name: RuntimeAndBoot
Phase-4.step-1: Wire M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME with oauth proxy for /mcp and mount admin routes.
Phase-4.step-2: Finalize M-SERVER/M-ROOT-ENTRYPOINT startup and shutdown lifecycle.
Phase-4.justification: Entrypoints are assembled last to avoid hidden coupling and ease smoke validation.

RiskAssessment.risk-1: Legacy requirements about API-key-based /mcp auth conflict with OAuth-proxy-only target and must be explicitly marked deprecated.
RiskAssessment.risk-2: OAuth callback/base URL misconfiguration can break authorization flow in production.
RiskAssessment.risk-3: Schema tightening may break existing client payloads that relied on permissive behavior.
RiskAssessment.risk-4: If /admin route mounting order is wrong, auth middleware can leak or over-protect endpoints.
RiskAssessment.risk-5: Tool description drift between Zod schema and docs can confuse MCP clients.

M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.NAME: McpAuthProvider
M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.reason: Replaced by FastMCP OAuth Proxy integration for /mcp.

M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.NAME: McpAuthAdapter
M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.reason: No longer needed when FastMCP OAuth Proxy handles OAuth session flow.

M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.NAME: ApiKeyRepository
M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.reason: Out of simplified runtime scope; admin remains ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN only.

M-DB.NAME: DrizzleDb
M-DB.reason: No active dependency in simplified runtime path.
